business_summary: Alphabet is the parent of Google. The vast majority of profit comes from Google Services: Search advertising (the single biggest piece), YouTube ads, the ad network, and subscriptions/devices (YouTube Premium, Google One, Pixel). Google Cloud is the fast-growing second engine, selling computing, data, and AI services to businesses. A small 'Other Bets' segment (e.g. Waymo) is still loss-making. In plain terms: Alphabet makes money mainly by showing ads next to the world's searches and videos, and increasingly by renting out its enormous AI/compute infrastructure to enterprises. FY2025 revenue was $402.8B (+15% YoY), with operating income of $129.0B (~32% operating margin) and net income of $132.2B.

moat.type: Network effects + data scale + intangibles (brand, AI/ML infrastructure, distribution). Google Search holds ~90% global share; more users -> more data -> better results -> more users. YouTube has an unmatched two-sided creator/viewer network. Google Cloud benefits from owning custom AI silicon (TPUs) and DeepMind/Gemini research. Distribution via Android, Chrome, and default-search deals reinforces the lead.

valuation.method: pe (normalized EPS ~$9.50 x 24/28/33) + fcf_yield (normalized FCF/sh ~$7.86 at 4.0/3.4/2.8%); FCF deliberately normalized because reported FCF is temporarily depressed by an extraordinary AI capex super-cycle ($91B capex in FY2025, guided $180-190B in FY2026)

bull_case: Wide-moat, cash-gushing core: FY2025 revenue $402.8B (+15%), operating income $129.0B (~32% margin), net income $132.2B, diluted EPS $10.81 (source: SEC Q4/FY2025 release).
Google Cloud is inflecting: Q1 2026 cloud revenue grew 63% YoY to $20.0B and turned solidly profitable; reported backlog nearly doubled QoQ to over $460B, signalling years of contracted demand.
Fortress balance sheet: ~$126.8B cash + marketable securities vs $46.5B long-term debt at end-2025 = ~$80B net cash, so the company can fund its AI buildout and keep buying back stock without strain.

Exceptional capital returns and economics: ROIC in the low-20s%+, ~60% gross margin, growing dividend ($0.22/qtr declared Apr 2026, +5%), and large ongoing buybacks reducing the share count (diluted shares ~12.24B in Q1 2026 vs ~12.29B a year earlier).
AI optionality is a two-way street: Gemini, custom TPUs, DeepMind, and Search/YouTube distribution give Alphabet a credible path to monetize generative AI rather than only be disrupted by it.
risks: AI disruption to Search: ChatGPT, Perplexity, Copilot and AI answer-engines could erode the query volume and click-driven ad revenue that funds most of Alphabet's profit. Search & other is the largest revenue line (~$54B/qtr run-rate), so even a few points of share/monetization loss is large in dollar terms.

Antitrust/legal: A U.S. court ruled Google illegally monopolized search; remedies (potential limits on default-search distribution deals, possible divestitures, conduct restrictions) could weaken the moat or cut high-margin revenue. Multiple cases (search, ad-tech) remain in remedy/appeal phases.

Capex super-cycle risk: FY2025 capex was $91.4B and FY2026 is guided to $180-190B, with FY2027 expected to rise further. If AI/cloud demand or returns disappoint, this depresses free cash flow and ROIC for years; reported FCF is already only ~$73B in FY2025 despite $164.7B operating cash flow.

Valuation/no margin of safety: at $335.79 the stock sits ~35% above our base fair value (~$249) and above the top of our fair-value band; that price already embeds strong continued AI/cloud success, so disappointment could de-rate the multiple.

Earnings volatility from equity stakes: reported net income/EPS are distorted by large unrealized gains/losses on equity securities (e.g. a ~$37.7B gain in Q1 2026), making headline EPS an unreliable valuation anchor.
what_would_break_thesis: Google Search & other (advertising) revenue declines year-over-year for two consecutive quarters, signalling real AI-driven share loss in the core profit engine.
Google Cloud revenue growth decelerates below ~20% YoY for two consecutive quarters AND the reported backlog stops growing, undermining the second-engine thesis.
A final antitrust remedy forces divestiture of a core asset (e.g. Chrome/Android) or bans the default-search distribution deals, materially impairing the moat.
Consolidated operating margin falls below 25% for two consecutive quarters (vs ~32% now), showing the capex/AI spend is not earning its keep.
Net cash position turns into net debt with net-debt-to-EBITDA rising above 1.0x, or a serious accounting/governance red flag emerges.
Annual free cash flow stays negative or near zero for a full fiscal year as capex outruns operating cash flow with no corresponding revenue payoff.
catalysts: Quarterly earnings (next ~late July 2026 for Q2 2026): proof points on Cloud growth/backlog and Search ad resilience vs AI.
Antitrust remedy rulings/appeals outcomes that clarify (positively or negatively) the future of default-search deals and ad-tech.
Gemini/AI product monetization milestones (AI Overviews ads, Gemini subscriptions, enterprise AI on Cloud) showing AI is additive to, not just cannibalizing, revenue.
Capex/return commentary: any signal that the AI buildout is translating into durable cloud revenue and improving returns on the spend.
